title: Platform overview
text: Nimbus Cloud Platform is a fictional cloud provider used as sample data. It offers compute, object storage, managed databases, networking, and observability services. Accounts are organized into projects, and every resource belongs to exactly one project.

title: Authentication and API keys
text: All API requests are authenticated with an API key sent in the X-Nimbus-Key header. Keys are created per project in the console under Settings, can be scoped to read-only or read-write, and can be rotated at any time. A key is shown only once at creation time.

title: Rate limits
text: The API enforces a default limit of 100 requests per second per project. When the limit is exceeded the API returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header. Clients should implement exponential backoff with jitter. Higher limits can be requested for production workloads.

title: Pricing model
text: Nimbus bills per second of compute usage and per gigabyte-month of storage. There are no upfront costs. Discounts apply automatically at sustained usage above 30 percent of the month. Invoices are issued monthly per project and can be exported as CSV.

title: Object storage
text: Nimbus Object Storage stores immutable blobs in buckets. Objects are replicated across three availability zones. Lifecycle rules can transition objects to cold storage after a configurable number of days or delete them. Maximum object size is 5 terabytes.

title: Compute instances
text: Compute instances come in general purpose, memory optimized, and GPU families. Instances boot from images and can be resized while stopped. Spot instances cost up to 80 percent less but can be reclaimed with a two minute warning.

title: Virtual networking
text: Each project gets an isolated virtual network. Subnets are zonal, firewall rules are stateful, and peering connects networks across projects. Load balancers distribute traffic across instances in multiple zones and perform health checks every 10 seconds.

title: Observability suite
text: The observability suite collects metrics, logs, and traces. Metrics are retained for 13 months, logs for 30 days by default. Alerting policies trigger notifications through email, webhooks, or the on-call integration when a condition holds for the configured duration.

title: Service level agreement
text: Nimbus offers a 99.95 percent monthly uptime SLA for compute and 99.99 percent for object storage. If uptime falls below the target, customers receive service credits between 10 and 50 percent of the monthly bill for the affected service, claimed within 30 days.

title: Compliance and data residency
text: Customer data is stored in the region selected at project creation and never leaves it. Nimbus is certified for SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001. Data is encrypted at rest with AES-256 and in transit with TLS 1.3. Customers can bring their own encryption keys.

title: Backups and disaster recovery
text: Managed databases take automatic daily snapshots retained for 7 days by default, configurable up to 35 days. Point-in-time recovery covers the last 24 hours. Cross-region replicas can be promoted manually during a regional outage.

title: Quotas and limits
text: New projects start with a quota of 32 vCPUs, 10 terabytes of storage, and 5 virtual networks. Quota increases are requested from the console and most are approved automatically within minutes. Quotas protect against runaway costs and accidental resource creation.
